title: erigeron parryi
intro: Erigeron parryi, also known as Parry's fleabane or daisy, is a perennial herbaceous flowering plant native to the western United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its leaves and roots. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and treating digestive issues due to its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Erigeron parryi can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
